description: The Plug Uglies were an American Nativist criminal street gang active in Baltimore, Maryland from 1854 to 1865. They were known for their involvement in election-day violence and street brawls, often using plug hats stuffed with wool as helmets. The gang participated in the 1856 Baltimore Know-Nothing Riot and the 1863 New York City Draft Riots. They were allied with the Know Nothing political party and rivaled Irish American gangs.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Baltimore, Maryland and Five Points, Manhattan, New York City, New York, present-day Worth Street, Baxter Street, and Columbus Park, in Manhattan, New York City, New York
country_links.context: The Plug Uglies operated in Baltimore, Maryland, and New York City, New York, within the United States.
